summary: Phantoms spawn during the first night on a world.
description: Phantoms will spawn in the overworld night sky regardless of the amount of time passed in the world. n nSteps to reproduce: n n1. Create a world n n2. Wait for the game to turn to night n n n nExpected result: Phantoms spawn at night if the player has not slept for three in-game days. n nActual result: Phantoms spawn on the first night if the player hasn t slept once. n n n n n n
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Create a new Survival world with default settings.
2. Do not sleep in a bed during the first day.
3. Collect basic resources and prepare for the night (e.g., gather wood, build a shelter).
4. Wait for the game to transition from day to night (approximately 8 minutes and 20 seconds after the world is created).
5. Remain outside or in an open area with sky access during the night.
6. Observe the night sky for the appearance of Phantoms after the night begins. 

Expected result: Phantoms should spawn at night if the player has not slept for three in-game days. 

Actual result: Phantoms spawn on the first night even if the player has not slept once.
Observed Behavior:
Actual Result:**
Phantoms unexpectedly spawn on the first night after creating a new world, even if the player has not slept at all. This behavior contradicts the intended mechanics, where phantoms should only begin to spawn after the player has gone three consecutive in-game days without sleeping. Upon entering the first night, phantoms appear in the overworld night sky regardless of the player's sleep status, leading to immediate and unanticipated encounters with these hostile mobs. The spawning occurs despite the fact that the player has not yet accumulated the required time of three in-game days without sleep. This results in players being unprepared for the sudden attack of phantoms, which can cause significant damage and disrupt gameplay.
Expected Behavior: Phantoms should only begin to spawn in the overworld night sky after the player has not slept for three consecutive in-game days. This means that once the player has entered a bed at least once, the "Time Since Last Rest" statistic resets, preventing phantoms from spawning until the player has gone without sleep for the required duration. The intended behavior is that on the first night, if the player has not slept, no phantoms should appear, allowing players to experience the initial night without the threat of these hostile mobs. After three in-game days without sleep, phantoms will spawn in increasing numbers, following the established mechanics of their spawning conditions.

comments.body_text:
Hi there!Could you please answer a few questions regarding this issue? As such we have no repro of this issue.

* Does this issue occur only on this world? If yes, please upload it here.


* Were there any game rules changed?



This ticket will automatically reopen when you reply. Thanks!
